challenges.title: Dropdown Menu
challenges.description: Create a select element with at least 5 option elements. One of the options should have the selected attribute to make it the default choice. Add a label for the select. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a dropdown menu with at least 5 options -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
lower = html.lower()
assert '<select' in lower, 'Should contain a <select> element'
assert '</select>' in lower, 'Should close the <select> element'
assert lower.count('<option') >= 5, 'Should contain at least 5 <option> elements'
assert 'selected' in lower, 'One option should have the selected attribute'
assert '<label' in lower, 'Should contain a <label> element'
hints: Add the selected attribute to one <option> to make it the default.
Each <option> can have a value attribute different from its display text.

challenges.title: Grouped Select
challenges.description: Create a select element that uses optgroup elements to group related options (e.g., group cars by manufacturer). Also create a separate input with a datalist element containing at least 3 options. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a grouped select and a datalist -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
lower = html.lower()
assert '<select' in lower, 'Should contain a <select> element'
assert '<optgroup' in lower, 'Should contain <optgroup> elements'
assert 'label=' in lower, 'Optgroup should have a label attribute'
assert lower.count('<optgroup') >= 2, 'Should have at least 2 option groups'
assert '</select>' in lower, 'Should close the <select> element'
assert '<datalist' in lower, 'Should contain a <datalist> element'
assert '</datalist>' in lower, 'Should close the <datalist> element'
assert '<input' in lower, 'Should contain an <input> element for the datalist'
assert 'list=' in lower, 'Input should have a list attribute referencing the datalist'
hints: The <optgroup> element uses a label attribute to name the group.
A <datalist> is linked to an <input> via the input's list attribute matching the datalist's id.
